sourceMovie.title: Kwaidan
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Kwaidan (1965) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Last Voyage of the Demeter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kwaidan clicked for you, The Last Voyage of the Demeter (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and fantasy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ugetsu
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kwaidan clicked for you, Ugetsu (1953) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Tokyo!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kwaidan clicked for you, Tokyo! (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kwaidan clicked for you, Pride & Prejudice (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Kwaidan?
faq.answer: Try The Last Voyage of the Demeter, Ugetsu, Tokyo!—each shares DNA with Kwaidan while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Kwaidan?
faq.answer: Kwaidan (1965) is best described as Horror, Fantasy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Kwaidan worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
